trajectory_summary: This call never connected to a live customer. The transcript is entirely automated voicemail system audio. There is no customer interaction, no qualification, no pitch, and no booking attempt to evaluate. The call's shape cannot be assessed on the energy_built / energy_leaked / mixed / stagnant axis because there was no live exchange — the only meaningful coaching question is whether the rep left an effective voicemail message, and that audio is not captured in this transcript.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is listed as 'unknown' — that is accurate. No appointment was booked. No customer was reached. The correct outcome classification is 'no contact / voicemail.' Any tag beyond that cannot be supported by this transcript.

primary_coaching_focus.quote: At the tone, please record your message. When you've finished recording, you may hang up, or press 1 for more options.
primary_coaching_focus.why: This call produced no evaluable setter behavior because the rep's spoken message is not captured in the transcript. Before coaching the rep on this specific call, the manager needs to ask: 'What did you say after the beep?' More broadly, Ion has no schema section covering voicemail strategy — and on paid leads where the bill is required before a sit can be set, the voicemail is the only re-engagement tool available. A standardized voicemail script (legitimacy hook, curiosity creation, specific callback ask) is a floor-wide gap worth raising with Kenny.

key_moments.quote: At the tone, please record your message. When you've finished recording, you may hang up, or press 1 for more options.
key_moments.stewart_read: The 51-second gap between this prompt and the first menu-repeat at 01:05 is the recording window. The rep's spoken message — if one was left — is entirely absent from this transcript. No claim about whether a message was left, what it said, or how it was framed can be made from the available evidence. This is a transcript-capture gap, not a rep behavior finding.

key_moments.quote: If you're satisfied with the message, press 1. To listen to your message, press 2. To erase and rerecord, press 3. To continue recording where you left off, press 4.
key_moments.stewart_read: The menu appears three times (01:05, 01:21, 01:37). Three cycles is consistent with a deliberate review-and-rerecord workflow — listen, decide to rerecord, confirm — as much as it is consistent with passive indecision. The transcript does not distinguish between these. No schema section covers voicemail navigation behavior.

The number reached is a (571) area code — Northern Virginia. This is a paid-lead context where the bill is required before an appointment can be set. A voicemail-only touch on a paid lead means the rep cannot advance the lead to a sit without a callback. The voicemail message — invisible in this transcript — is the only tool the rep had to generate that callback. Its quality is therefore directly tied to whether this lead converts to a sit.

key_moments.quote: Your call has been forwarded to an automated voice messaging system.
key_moments.stewart_read: The entire call is automated system audio. There is no customer interaction, no qualification data, no emotional arc, and no booking attempt. This transcript cannot be evaluated on any of the standard setter-call dimensions (verify completion, bill anchor, spouse handling, etc.). The only evaluable question — voicemail message quality — is not captured in the transcript format used here.
